title: Got diagnosed w/ L1 Autism for 2ndX so talking Neurotypical vs Autistics as an official Autistic

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares that he was just diagnosed with Level 1 autism for the second time, and uses this as a springboard to explain his own theory of how autistic minds work differently from neurotypical minds — emphasizing triangulation, big-picture thinking, and insatiable curiosity over rote memorization. He covers his personal history of not fitting in, failed friendships, his history timeline project, and how he believes autistic people are mentally ahead of neurotypicals.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Not into labels, finds it awkward to identify as autistic even after two diagnoses
Autistics are more logical and future-oriented; neurotypicals rely on primitive social signaling (non-verbal cues) because they lack substantive content
Autistics notice them but ignore them on purpose to stay focused on actual data
Results from extreme concentration on one narrow domain at the expense of everything else, like Rain Man
Fails autistic people by forcing sequential, slow learning instead of holistic, self-paced discovery
Autistics make the mistake of befriending people unlike themselves and end up being used; better to focus on self-improvement
Believes it's achievable and worthwhile; hopes autistic people will embrace his timeline project
Autistics are better — more ambitious, more capable of deep focus — but disadvantaged by a neurotypical-majority world

entities: Miyamoto Musashi (referenced loosely as 'Moshihara' / Book of Five Rings)
